mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 9.5%, deteriorating labor market conditions compress consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates a supply-demand imbalance that forces inflationary pressures, prompting monetary tightening that depresses equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of out-of-sample instances, or the cross-correlation between INDPRO and HOUST shows no predictive power for SPX over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising core prices force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPI exceeds 215 and rates remain above 0.2%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during disinflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases, improving earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as equity markets price in stronger economic activity.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth rates, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in payroll employment exceeding 200,000 new jobs per month signals robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of triggered months, or the rank correlation between monthly payroll changes and subsequent 5-day SPX returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises alongside falling real consumer credit, corporate capex outpaces household borrowing, leading to a credit contraction that restricts future demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of out-of-sample instances, or RSAFS declines do not precede SPX drawdowns when INDPRO remains elevated.

mechanism_narrative: During housing market downturns, declining housing starts act as a leading indicator for broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors price in reduced construction and consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts fall below 550, or the signal's predictive power disappears when adjusted for concurrent GDP growth trends.
